title: Opening Verbiage
content:
Greeting


Standard opening:


"Hello, thank you for contacting Tklesson! My name is [Agent Name]. How may I assist you today?"


Let the customer explain their issue fully before jumping in. Active listening builds trust.



Empathy Follow-Ups


Once the customer describes their problem, acknowledge their frustration before moving to solutions:


  • "I understand how frustrating it can be to [not be able to access your account / lose a session credit / wait for a refund]. I'm happy to assist you with this."
  • "Thank you for bringing this to our attention. Let me look into this for you right away."
  • "I can see why that would be concerning. Let's get this sorted out together."


Determine If Security Checks Are Needed


Security checks required:


  • Password reset requests
  • Disabling 2FA
  • Account recovery
  • Credit adjustments or refund requests
  • Any changes to account details

No security checks needed:


  • General questions about services or pricing
  • Information about rewards and offers
  • How-to questions about using the platform
  • Competition details and eligibility inquiries


Non-Tklesson Callers ("Muggles")


If the caller is not a registered Tklesson user, take only their first name and append (Muggle) to identify them as a non-platform contact. This helps track inquiries from prospective clients versus active users.


Example: "John (Muggle) called to ask about our tutoring rates and whether we cover AP Calculus."


If a Muggle expresses interest in signing up, direct them to tklesson.com to create an account or book a free consultation. Do not collect personal information beyond their first name.



Setting the Tone


  • Always use a warm, professional tone. The customer may already be frustrated — your calm demeanor sets the pace.
  • Never rush the conversation. Even if you know the answer immediately, make the customer feel heard.
  • If you need to look something up, say: "Give me just a moment while I pull up your account."
  • If the issue requires escalation, say: "I want to make sure this is handled correctly. Let me document this and our team will follow up within 24 hours."
